Phase 9: Curriculum Review & Iteration (phase execution)
coverage_matrix.notes: Coverage is strong for happy path, edge cases, and primary error handlers. Phase 9 (Curriculum Review & Iteration) is not covered by any test case as it requires post-publication learner feedback. Some error handlers for persona identification, skill level differentiation, and page limits are not explicitly tested but are addressed through quality gate validation.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill executes all 9 phases successfully for a typical technical topic with standard inputs, producing all 7 required outputs.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Complete end-to-end curriculum generation with all phases, all outputs, learner personas, prerequisite self-assessment, and capstone project.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Missing phases, incomplete outputs, missing learner persona mapping, missing capstone project, missing prerequisite remediation, incomplete learning objectives.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill handles non-technical domains (creative arts) as well as technical domains, ensuring domain-agnostic curriculum design.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Curriculum generation for creative/non-technical topic with appropriate learning objectives, exercises, and resources for hobbyist audience.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Domain-specific bias toward technical topics, inability to generate meaningful learning objectives for creative domains, inappropriate resource recommendations for non-technical audiences.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill gracefully handles minimal input (only required topic) and uses sensible defaults for all optional inputs.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Default value handling for target_audience, domain_context, time_constraints, and resource_preferences when not provided.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Missing default values, null/undefined outputs when optional inputs are omitted, skill failure on minimal input.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill detects and appropriately handles very narrow/niche topics that cannot be meaningfully decomposed into 5-12 core concepts.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error detection and adjustment for narrow topics, suggestion to combine with related topics or treat as single module.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Failure to detect narrow scope, forcing decomposition of topics that are too specific, producing artificial or meaningless core concepts.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill detects and appropriately handles extremely broad topics that cannot be meaningfully bounded.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Error detection and adjustment for overly broad topics, request for scope narrowing or suggestion to split into multiple curricula.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Failure to detect overly broad scope, producing unfocused or incomplete curricula, attempting to cover too many concepts.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill detects and resolves circular dependencies in prerequisites, which would otherwise block curriculum execution.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Circular dependency detection, bootstrap concept identification, parallel learning paths, orientation phase creation.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Undetected circular dependencies, infinite prerequisite loops, invalid prerequisite ordering, skill failure on circular input.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill gracefully handles cases where insufficient quality resources exist for a module (emerging/niche topics).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Resource availability detection, fallback to community resources, documentation of gaps, curriculum completion despite resource limitations.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Skill failure when resources are unavailable, incomplete resource lists, failure to document gaps, inability to suggest alternatives.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that skill levels are meaningfully differentiated with distinct, non-overlapping learning objectives at each level.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Skill level differentiation, SMART format learning objectives, no overlap between levels, clear progression from Beginner to Expert.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Overlapping learning objectives across levels, vague or non-SMART objectives, indistinguishable skill levels, missing Expert level.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that learning path modules are appropriately sized (1-3 concepts, 4-8 hours) and sequenced with explicit dependencies.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Module concept count, module duration, explicit prerequisite dependencies, real-world applications per module.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Oversized modules (>3 concepts or >8 hours), undersized modules (<1 concept or <4 hours), missing dependencies, missing real-world applications.

test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that assessment criteria are aligned with learning objectives and use measurable passing thresholds (70% knowledge + 80% exercises).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Assessment-to-objective alignment, knowledge check design, practical task design, measurable passing thresholds, assessment coverage for all levels.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Misaligned assessments, vague passing criteria, missing assessments for some levels, non-measurable assessment criteria, threshold not meeting 70%+80% standard.

chain_definition.name: curriculum-designer-chain
chain_definition.description: Design a structured, multi-level learning curriculum from any topic with prerequisites, learning path, resources, exercises, and assessments.

entry_inputs.topic: string (required) - The subject or skill to design a curriculum for
entry_inputs.target_audience: string (optional) - Who will follow this curriculum
entry_inputs.domain_context: string (optional) - Industry, field, or specialization context
entry_inputs.time_constraints: string (optional) - Total time available for curriculum completion

coverage_report.goal: Take any topic and generate a structured curriculum: prerequisites, learning path, resources, practice exercises, and assessment criteria for 4 skill levels.

requirements.requirement: Decompose topic into core concepts and learning objectives
Identify learner personas and differentiate learning paths
Define prerequisites and create self-assessment tools
covered_by: curriculum-designer (Phase 2: Prerequisites & Foundation Mapping, Phase 2.5: Prerequisite Self-Assessment & Remediation)

requirements.requirement: Create 4-level skill framework with SMART learning objectives
covered_by: curriculum-designer (Phase 3: Skill Level Framework & Learning Objectives)

requirements.requirement: Design sequenced learning path with 6-12 modules
Curate high-quality, current resources for each module
Design hands-on practice exercises with success criteria
Create assessment criteria and passing thresholds for each level
Design capstone project integrating core concepts
Synthesize all outputs into cohesive curriculum document
Enable curriculum iteration based on learner feedback

execution_plan.title: Curriculum Designer Execution Plan
execution_plan.description: Single-step pipeline executing the curriculum-designer skill to produce a complete, structured curriculum from any topic.
execution_plan.estimated_total_duration: Depends on topic complexity and available resources; typically 40-80 hours for comprehensive curriculum design
execution_plan.parallelization_opportunity: No parallelization possible; curriculum-designer is a monolithic skill. However, internally it executes 9 sequential phases with quality gates at each phase.

execution_layers.description: Execute curriculum-designer skill with topic and audience inputs. Produces 7 granular outputs: curriculum_overview, prerequisites, skill_levels, learning_path, resources, practice_exercises, assessment_criteria.
execution_layers.estimated_duration: 40-80 hours (depending on topic complexity, resource availability, and learner feedback cycles)

quality_gates.gate: Each core concept has unique definition and is teachable in <4 hours; relationships are explicit; scope is bounded (5-12 concepts)
3-5 distinct personas identified; each has documented learning preferences and barriers; curriculum includes resources/exercises for each persona
Each prerequisite named with specific skill; no circular dependencies; learners can realistically acquire prerequisites
Self-assessment tool is objective and <15 minutes; each common gap has 2+ remediation resources; resources are accessible/free

quality_gates.phase: Phase 3: Skill Level Framework & Learning Objectives
quality_gates.gate: Each level has distinct objectives (no overlaps); objectives in SMART format; durations realistic and justified
Dependencies explicit; each module covers 1-3 concepts, completable in 4-8 hours; path learnable within time constraints; 2+ real-world applications per module
Resources current (3 years), reputable, 4+ stars; all learner personas covered; resources accessible/affordable
Exercises practical and tied to objectives; success criteria objective/testable; common misconceptions and troubleshooting documented; all personas covered
Assessments aligned with objectives; passing threshold 70%+ knowledge + 80%+ exercises; progression criteria clear/measurable
Capstone integrates 5+ core concepts; requirements clear/measurable; rubric objective and aligned with Expert-level objectives
All sections present/complete; cross-references accurate; document clear/usable; no orphaned learning objectives
Feedback collected from 3+ learners; updates documented with rationale; version incremented; review process documented

exit_criteria_checklist: ✓ All four skill levels defined with SMART learning objectives
✓ Complete learning path with 6-12 sequenced modules
✓ Each module has 3+ curated resources (current, reputable, reviewed) and 2-3 practice exercises
✓ Assessment criteria and passing thresholds (70%+ knowledge + 80%+ exercises) defined for each level
✓ Capstone project integrating 5+ core concepts designed
✓ Learner personas mapped to resources and exercises
✓ Prerequisite self-assessment tool and remediation resources provided
✓ Common misconceptions and troubleshooting guides documented for each exercise
✓ Learner unfamiliar with topic could follow curriculum and achieve stated objectives
✓ Total estimated duration realistic and validated against learner feedback or pilot data
✓ All cross-references accurate and complete
✓ Curriculum tested with 3+ learners from target audience

notes.skill_analysis: The 'curriculum-designer' skill is a comprehensive, well-structured meta-skill that handles the entire curriculum design process end-to-end. It is self-contained and requires no upstream skills.
notes.chain_structure: This is a single-node chain with no dependencies. The curriculum-designer skill is the sole executor. All curriculum generation happens within this single skill's 9 sequential phases.
notes.modularity: The improved skill definition breaks the monolithic output into 7 granular outputs (curriculum_overview, prerequisites, skill_levels, learning_path, resources, practice_exercises, assessment_criteria), enabling downstream skills to consume specific curriculum components independently.
notes.quality_improvements: The skill definition has been significantly improved with: (1) measurable quality gates replacing vague language, (2) new phases for learner personas (1.5), prerequisite self-assessment (2.5), capstone design (7.5), and curriculum iteration (9), (3) state persistence for tracking curriculum versions and learner feedback, (4) comprehensive error handling for all phases, (5) SMART learning objectives format guidance, (6) learner persona types and resource type guidance in reference section.
notes.execution_recommendation: To use this chain, provide a topic and target audience as entry inputs. The curriculum-designer skill will execute all 9 phases internally, producing a complete, validated curriculum ready for publication or pilot testing. For iterative improvement, re-run Phase 9 after collecting learner feedback.
